name: Vía Verde de El Ronquillo - Water Route - circular
name_original: Vía Verde de El Ronquillo - Ruta del Agua – circular
region_intro: Touring cycling around El Ronquillo offers routes through varied landscapes, characterized by rolling hills, dense Mediterranean forests, and the presence of significant reservoirs. The region's terrain often includes sections of former railway lines converted into greenways, providing smoother paths for cyclists. These routes frequently navigate through natural parks and protected areas, showcasing the local flora and fauna. The elevation changes provide both challenging climbs and rewarding descents, typical of the Sierra Morena foothills. Best touring cycling routes around El Ronquillo The most popular touring cycling route is Vía Verde de El Ronquillo - Water Route - circular, a 28.7 miles (46.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 37 minutes to complete. This circular route follows a former railway line, offering a consistent gradient and scenic views related to water features. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is La Minilla Reservoir – La Cantina loop from El Ronquillo, a difficult 42.5 miles (68.5 km) path. This route encircles the La Minilla Reservoir, providing extensive views of the water and surrounding forested hills. Local touring cyclists also love the Almadén de la Plata – Cerro del Calvario Viewpoint loop from, a 30.2 miles (48.6 km) trail leading through hilly terrain to the Cerro del Calvario Viewpoint, often completed in about 4 hours 51 minutes. Touring cycling around El Ronquillo is defined by greenways, reservoir landscapes, and forested hills, offering a mix of moderate and difficult routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes present significant elevation gains. The routes in El Ronquillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore El Ronquillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around El Ronquillo?
A: There are 15 touring cycling routes available around El Ronquillo. Most of these routes are classified as difficult, with 3 moderate options and no easy routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around El Ronquillo?
A: The terrain around El Ronquillo is characterized by rolling hills, dense Mediterranean forests, and significant reservoirs. Many routes utilize former railway lines converted into greenways, offering smoother paths. You'll encounter both challenging climbs and rewarding descents typical of the Sierra Morena foothills.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in El Ronquillo?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988052742/via-verde-de-el-ronquillo-water-route-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de El Ronquillo - Water Route - circular&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, following a former railway line. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20126294" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Minilla Reservoir – La Cantina loop from El Ronquillo&lt;/a&gt;, which encircles the reservoir.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for touring cycling routes in El Ronquillo?
A: Routes vary in length and time. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988052742/via-verde-de-el-ronquillo-water-route-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de El Ronquillo - Water Route - circular&lt;/a&gt; is about 28.7 miles (46.1 km) and takes around 3 hours 37 minutes. Longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20126294" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Minilla Reservoir – La Cantina loop from El Ronquillo&lt;/a&gt; cover 42.5 miles (68.5 km) and can take nearly 6 hours.

Q: What can I see along the touring cycling routes around El Ronquillo?
A: Many routes offer scenic views of the region's reservoirs, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2710424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Minilla Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3967733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5084729" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rivera de Huelva&lt;/a&gt; or explore trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4081845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Solitary Rural Road&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, some routes lead to notable viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24522769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén de la Plata – Cerro del Calvario Viewpoint loop&lt;/a&gt;, for example, takes you through hilly terrain to the Cerro del Calvario Viewpoint, offering expansive vistas.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy about cycling in El Ronquillo?
A: The komoot community rates the routes highly, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic greenways, and the beautiful reservoir landscapes that define the area.

Q: Are there less crowded touring cycling routes for a more tranquil experience?
A: For a quieter experience, consider routes that venture deeper into the natural parks and protected areas, away from the main greenways. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31860334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de las Guardas – Scenic Rocky Path loop from Restaurante caserío Serranito&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging ride through varied landscapes that can be less frequented.

Q: Are there any places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: While specific bike-friendly cafes aren't detailed for every route, you might find establishments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2703687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cantina&lt;/a&gt;, which is featured on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20126294" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Minilla Reservoir – La Cantina loop&lt;/a&gt;, providing a potential stop. It's always advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in El Ronquillo?
A: The region experiences hot summers, so spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for touring cycling. During these seasons, the weather is milder, making longer rides more enjoyable.

Q: Is it possible to access El Ronquillo's cycling routes using public transport?
A: El Ronquillo is a small town, and while there might be limited bus services connecting it to larger cities like Seville, direct public transport access to specific trailheads with bike carriage facilities can be challenging. It's often more convenient to arrive by car, especially with a touring bicycle.

Q: Where can I find parking near the trailheads in El Ronquillo?
A: Parking is generally available within El Ronquillo itself, particularly near the start points of popular routes like the Vía Verde. For routes that begin further afield, such as those around the reservoirs, look for designated parking areas or suitable roadside spots near the access points.
tips: Vía Verde de El Ronquillo passes next to this beautiful reservoir. It is a perfect place to spend the day with the family, barbecue or fish.

The track links the old El Ronquillo railway station and the Seville-Huelva provincial border and has four rest areas. On the route there are some gates to prevent the passage of cattle, remember to always leave them closed!
The reservoir located in the natural park of the Sierra Norte de Sevilla in a natural environment known as Lagos del Serrano. Built in 1927.
